subject: Geography
title: Bat Colonies
description: A quick win: understand Bat Colonies.

hook.text: Bracken Cave in Texas hosts 20 million Mexican free-tailed bats—the largest bat colony on Earth.

buildup.text: Each night, the colony eats 140 tons of insects, including crop-destroying moths.

discovery.text: Mother bats find their own pup among millions by memorizing its unique call and scent.

twist.text: White-nose syndrome, a fungal disease, has killed millions of bats across North America since 2006.

climax.text: Losing bats means losing pest control worth billions of dollars to agriculture.

punchline.text: The world's best pest controllers live in caves—and they're disappearing.

quiz.question: How many bats live in Bracken Cave?
